content:
## Dry-Run vs Live Mode

Dry-run means the write node is a no-op — classification runs and proposals are written to `staging/dry-run/` instead of being applied. In live mode, proposals go to `staging/pending/` for auto-commit to the wiki. The distinction is a flag, not a separate pipeline. You can inspect every proposal in dry-run and see exactly what the tree would have done before trusting it to run live. [[source:811302f6-0be7-435c-b844-910cc9a21b67/18]]

provenance.raw_agent: Dry-run just means the write node is a no-op. The classification runs, the proposal is written to staging/dry-run/ instead of being applied. You can inspect every proposal and see what the tree would have done. When you trust it, you flip a flag and proposals go to staging/pending/ for auto-commit.
provenance.raw_user: I think we should build some of the ground work for running the behaviour tree as a dry run.
